Parameters and loss are key concepts introduced in the context of optimization algorithms.
SPSA and ZO-SGD are different optimization algorithms discussed in the context.
SPSA can be used in more complex optimizers like Adam, as mentioned in the context.
SPSA is an acronym for Simultaneous Perturbation Stochastic Approximation, which involves estimating the gradient on a minibatch using perturbation and random sampling.
SPSA estimates the gradient on a minibatch based on the parameters of the model.
SPSA estimates the gradient on a minibatch based on the loss function of the model.
SPSA requires only two forward passes through the model to compute the gradient estimate, with the number of forward passes depending on the value of n in n-SPSA.
The SPSA estimate can be understood as a rank-1 reconstruction of the gradient as the perturbation scale approaches 0.
The SPSA estimate can replace the backpropagation gradient in any optimizer such as SGD, making it widely applicable in optimization algorithms.
The original SPSA algorithm perturbs the model by 1/z and requires that z has finite inverse moments, affecting the choice of z as a Gaussian in the perturbation process.
The loss function of the model is based on the parameters used in the model.
The gradient estimate in SPSA is based on the loss function of the model, which influences the computation of the gradient on a minibatch.
The number of forward passes in SPSA impacts the gradient estimate and the overall efficiency of the algorithm.
Backpropagation is a technique used in neural networks to calculate the gradient of the loss function with respect to the weights of the network.
Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD) is a popular optimization algorithm used to minimize the loss function in machine learning models.
ZO-SGD is a specific optimizer that updates parameters based on the Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD) algorithm.
MeZO is a memory-efficient implementation of the ZO-SGD optimizer as described in Algorithm 1.
The SPSA gradient estimate is used in the ZO-SGD algorithm to update parameters based on the gradient of the loss function.
Parameters are updated in the ZO-SGD optimizer using the Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD) algorithm.
Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) techniques are used to fine-tune a fraction of the network parameters to reduce storage costs of fine-tuned model checkpoints.
